idea.hook: Look up at the night sky. What you see is only a tiny fraction of reality.
facts: All 'normal matter' (stars, planets, gas, YOU) accounts for just 4.9% of the Universe's total mass-energy.
Mysterious 'Dark Matter' makes up about 26.8%, pulling galaxies together but emitting no light.
Even more mysterious 'Dark Energy' makes up roughly 68.3%, actively pushing the Universe apart.
idea.payoff: The vast majority (95.1%) of the cosmos is made of substances we cannot see or directly detect!
idea.title: The Universe is 95% Invisible! 🤯🌌

scenes.visual: A stunning, high-resolution shot of a dense star field in a dark night sky, possibly with a faint nebula or galaxy in the distance.

A dark, empty space background. A simple pie chart fades in, showing only a tiny 4.9% slice highlighted, labeled 'NORMAL MATTER' with a small star/planet icon. The rest of the circle is dark and empty.

The pie chart from scene 2. A new, larger 26.8% slice slides in and fills the chart, labeled 'DARK MATTER'. It has a subtle, swirling, ghostly texture and appears to gravitationally 'pull' on the 'NORMAL MATTER' slice slightly.

The pie chart from scene 3. The final 68.3% slice slides in to complete the circle, labeled 'DARK ENERGY'. It has a subtle, expanding or pushing-out animation, with faint arrows pointing outwards from its segment. The background subtly expands as well.

The completed pie chart, starkly illustrating the massive 'DARK MATTER' and 'DARK ENERGY' portions against the tiny 'NORMAL MATTER' slice. A large, transparent question mark subtly overlays the entire dark portion of the chart. The background is a vast, dark, cosmic void.
